title: Classes and Objects
ques: Create a class `Car` with fields `color` and `maxSpeed`. Instantiate an object of `Car` and set its fields.
explanation: A class is a blueprint for objects. `new Car()` creates an instance (object) of the class.

title: Constructors
ques: Add a **constructor** to the `Car` class to initialize `color` and `maxSpeed` upon object creation.
explanation: Constructors have the same name as the class and no return type. They run when an object is created.

title: Access Modifiers
ques: Explain the difference between `public` and `private`. Create a class with a **private** field and try to access it from `Main` (show it fails or explain why).
explanation: `public` members are accessible from anywhere. `private` members are restricted to the containing class.

title: Properties (Getters/Setters)
ques: Use a **Property** with get and set accessors to encapuslate a private field `name`. Ensure `name` is not empty in the setter.
explanation: Properties provide a flexible mechanism to read, write, or compute the value of a private field, often adding validation logic.

title: Auto-Implemented Properties
ques: Rewrite the previous property using **Auto-Implemented Property** syntax for a `Models` property in the `Car` class.
explanation: Auto-implemented properties ` { get; set; }` make code concise when no special logic is needed in accessors. The compiler creates a hidden backing field.

title: Inheritance
ques: Create a base class `Vehicle` with a method `Honk`. Create a derived class `Car` that **inherits** from `Vehicle`.
explanation: Inheritance (`:`) allows sharing code. `Car` inherits fields and methods from `Vehicle`.

title: Polymorphism (Virtual/Override)
ques: Use **`virtual`** and **`override`** to enable polymorphism. `Animal` class makes a sound, `Pig` overrides it to say "Wee Wee".
explanation: `virtual` allows a method to be effectively overridden in a derived class. `override` provides the new implementation.

title: Abstraction (Abstract Classes)
ques: Define an **abstract class** `Shape` with an abstract method `Area`. Implement `Area` in a `Square` subclass.
explanation: Abstract classes cannot be instantiated. Abstract methods have no body and must be implemented by derived classes.

title: Interfaces
ques: Create an **interface** `IAnimal` with a method `animalSound()`. Implement it in a `Dog` class.
explanation: Interfaces contain only abstract methods and properties (implicitly public). A class `implements` an interface using `:`.

title: Static Members
ques: Add a **static** field `count` to a class `User` to track the number of users created. Increment it in the constructor.
explanation: Static members belong to the class itself, not any specific object. They are shared across all instances.
